POSITION DESCRIPTION - SUPERVISOR
Reportable to Site Senior Executive

MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Undertake quarrying and sales activities to achieve key objectives in the areas of Customer Service, Quality, Workplace Health and Safety and Environmental Management.
Conduct and record prestart meeting when required.
Direct and supervise daily roles of the workforce.
Ensure that all workers (including contractors) have the appropriate skills, expertise and competencies to undertake the tasks and activities delegated to them.
Ensure loader operators are aware of correct loading requirements and weights of trucks, to ensure that overloading does not occur.
Be aware of the Chain of Responsibility Policy.
Provide appropriate training to ensure all workers (including contractors) comply with the organisations quality, safety and environmental standards and systems.
Carry out checks to ensure contractor plant and equipment is fit for purpose as per your obligations under Contractor Management.
Ensure policies, procedures and objectives are implemented and observed within the operation.
Write purchase orders as required.
Act as spare man and operate machines when usual operator is absent.
Operate pugmill when required.
Supervise and/or conduct/assist with repairs or maintenance on crushing equipment.
Operate water truck for dust suppression when required.
Visually monitor your surrounds at all times and report any concerns immediately.
Assist Site Senior Executive with quarry development.
Assist Site Senior Executive with task preparation and planning and development of Safe Work Procedures.
Secondary support to Site Senior Executive and Weighbridge Controller in his/her absence.
Other duties as directed or required within your field of competency.

QUALIFICATIONS
Competencies recognised by the Mining Safety and Health Advisory Committee (MQSHA 1999 and MQSHR 2017):
RIIRIS301E Apply risk management processes
RIIWHS301E Conduct safety and health investigations
RIICOM301E Communicate information

SKILLS
Proven hands on experience in fixed and mobile crushing/screening and quarrying.
Strong mechanical aptitude.
Proven leadership qualities as well as a strong commitment to safety.
Self-motivated, hardworking, reliable and a team player.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Computer skills including MS Office products.

PERFORMANCE GOALS
Ensure all workers are working both efficiently and safely at all times.
Interact with Management and other Employees with a friendly manner at all times.

BHP is a world-leading resources company. We work in more than 90
locations worldwide and our products are sold globally.\
\
We\'ve positioned our business to support the megatrends shaping our
world. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global
infrastructure and the energy transition. Copper for renewable energy.
And we are moving into potash to support more sustainable farming.\
\
A resource mix for today - and critical to the future.

About BHP

At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and
reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a
diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career
opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work
environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values
and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our
success.

About BHP Operations Services

BHP Operations Services delivers industry leading mining services across
BHP operations Australia-wide with specialist capabilities in load and
haul, fixed plant maintenance and mobile maintenance.

About the Roles

BHP is seeking motivated and safety-focused leaders to join our
Operations Services team in various leadership positions, including
Supervisor and Superintendent roles. These positions play a vital role
in driving team performance, maintaining safety excellence, and
supporting continuous improvement across our operations.

You\'ll be based at one of our Queensland sites --- Saraji, Caval Ridge,
Goonyella Riverside, or Peak Downs --- depending on operational
requirements and your area of expertise.

-   Supervisor positions: 7 days on, 7 days off, 7 nights on, 7 nights
    off roster

-   Superintendent positions: 5 days on, 2 days off roster

As part of our leadership team, you\'ll be responsible for supporting
and empowering your team to deliver outstanding results while fostering
an inclusive, respectful, and high‑performing culture.

About You

You\'re a people‑focused leader who thrives on mentoring and developing
others. You value safety above all, communicate effectively, and build
strong working relationships across all levels of the business. Your
background in a similar operational or leadership environment will
enable you to contribute meaningfully to our team\'s success.

Key Accountabilities:

-   Lead and support your team to achieve operational targets safely and
    efficiently

-   Foster a positive and productive culture built on trust, respect,
    and accountability

-   Promote and uphold BHP\'s HSEC standards, including proactive hazard
    management

-   Identify and implement opportunities to improve performance,
    processes, and systems

-   Collaborate cross‑functionally to support continuous improvement and
    business outcomes

-   Drive asset reliability and operational efficiency through effective
    maintenance strategies

-   Coach, mentor, and develop team members to reach their full
    potential

-   Utilise systems such as SAP to plan, manage, and report on
    operational performance

-   A trade qualification or relevant tertiary qualification is highly
    desirable, along with demonstrated leadership experience in mining,
    maintenance, or other heavy industry sectors.

Why BHP?

You\'ll be part of a company that values its people, putting safety and
inclusion at the forefront of everything we do. Working alongside
supportive leaders, you\'ll have the opportunity to build your
leadership capability, drive business improvement, and create a

pathway for continued career growth within BHP.

Be part of something bigger --- join us in shaping the future of our
operations.

About Our Process

At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP
Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the
recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted
to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police /
criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due
diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your
BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.

Supporting a Diverse Workforce

The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides
significant opportunity for professional development, but also
attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a
best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of
our employees\' lives that are important, and work is only one of these,
so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These
benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental
leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms. 

At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal
Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and
inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every
day. We are focused on creating a workforce that\'s more diverse and
represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work
environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with
respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true
diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual
orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more
aspects of your identity.\
\
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair,
equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that
it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for
your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone
about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us
at inclusion@bhp.com.

Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:

Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects
in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown

increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the

customers\' objectives to create a lasting positive impact. We serve the
Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security & Environmental; Energy; Mining &
Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span

believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver. Learn more about

Bechtel Mining and Metals offers engineering, procurement, and
construction services for customers in the mining sector across multiple
commodities including Iron Ore, Bauxite, Alumina, Aluminium, Copper,
Gold, Coal, Lithium and Rare Earths markets.The M&M global business unit
also has offices in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide in Australia along with
execution units in the United States, Peru, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia,
India and other project locations around the world.

Job Summary:

The Supervisor for site services is responsible for supervising and
directing operations within the in directs and site support services for
construction. Supervises and directs temporary facility and indirect
craft professionals, subordinate contractor services, and others, as
assigned. Ensures assigned construction performance is following
specifications and within schedules and budgets.

\
This role is offered on a FIFO basis to North Queensland, operating on a
2:1 roster

Major Responsibilities:

-   Implementing the project ES&H program and quality control program.

-   Supervises, coordinates and/or directs craft activities as assigned
    for the services disciplines.

-   Coordinating craft safety training and always enforcing safe work
    practices in conjunction with the ES&H staff.

-   Planning the craft manpower and interfacing with Industrial
    Relations for craft recruitment, hiring, and skills training
    programs.

-   Oversight of the needs of the Site services crews for craft
    personnel, subcontractor support, construction services,
    construction equipment, tools, and consumables as well as optimizing
    their utilization.

-   Working closely with other lead discipline superintendents,
    supervisors and coordinating efforts and activities to meet the
    overall project requirements.

-   Maintaining a high level of housekeeping throughout the work area.

Education and Experience Requirements:

-   10 years of related large industrial construction project services
    support experience.

-   5 years of supervisory experience in a site services function role
    with experience in managing and maintain temporary facilities, waste
    management, traffic control and road and site access.

Required Knowledge and Skills:

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of construction operations at
    the jobsite

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of the requirements of the
    Bechtel Environmental, Safety & Health  Core Processes and
    Bechtel's "Zero Accidents" philosophy.

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of Bechtel\'s Standard Work
    Process Procedures .

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of Bechtel Standard
    Application Program  standards and other automation tools
    which are used on Bechtel projects.

-   Must be computer literate in word processing, spreadsheet, and
    database applications in accordance with current BSAP standards.

-   Must be familiar with current industry quality standards and
    applicable code requirements.

Total Rewards/Benefits:

For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of
employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world\'s
toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people
thrive. Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance
our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to
build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total
Rewards

Diverse teams build the extraordinary:

As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude
of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This

because they feel safe, included and empowered.

Learn more about working at Thiess.

The opportunity:

Roster: 14/14 day/night shift rotating roster \| Location: Eva Copper,
Queensland \| Employment Type: Full time

Are you a Mining Supervisor with a passion for operational excellence
and innovative solutions? Thiess is seeking a highly skilled and
motivated Mining Supervisor to join our dynamic team at the Eva
Copper Mine Project in the Cloncurry region of Queensland, Australia. In
this role, you will play a key part in delivering safe, efficient, and
sustainable mining operations, driving technical excellence and
supporting project success.

As a key member of the Eva Copper Alliance team, you'll be responsible
for delivering high-quality mine planning and mine engineering processes
to support safe, efficient operations and ensure our workforce meets
Thiess and industry standards.

Working an 14/14 rotating day/night roster and reporting to the Mining
Manager, your duties will include:

-   Build and maintain relationships with all stakeholders not limited
    to but including Technical Services and Maintenance teams

-   Manage, report, and investigate all incidents within the mining
    operation and apply corrective actions at the level of
    responsibility

-   Complete safety observations and critical control audits within own
    work area against current work procedures

-   Execution of weekly plan and be able to highlight any shortfalls
    within the plan that have the potential to disrupt operations

-   Attend the daily production meeting and be able to provide an
    accurate summary of the previous 24 hrs of production

-   Add value and support our \"Speak Up\" culture, encouraging
    employees to live out and align with expected behaviours 

-   Support and development of operators ensuring competencies are kept
    up to date and individuals are fully aware of their responsibilities
    within the workplace

About you

As a successful applicant, you'll be a high-achieving team-focused
individual who enjoys delivering results in a dynamic environment. Your
exceptional relationship-building skills coupled with your ability to
engage and collaborate effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders
will also ensure your success in this role.

-   Proven leadership and people management skills within an adaptive
    team

-   Excellent organisational, communication, and presentation skills
    coupled with approachable and motivational qualities

-   Prioritising and problem-solving are second nature

-   A solid understanding of the processes involved with hard rock
    mining

What Thiess can do for you

At Thiess, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We're
committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive --- safely,
confidently and with opportunities to grow.

-   Help to build on your career, offering diverse opportunities to
    expand your skill set

-   Salary sacrifice options

How to apply:**

This role is an opportunity to take the next step in your career. With

work environment, there is no better time to join Thiess and the Eva
Copper Alliance.

ABOUT US

PYBAR -- a Thiess Company, is a mining services provider with core
competencies in underground hard rock mining. With origins dating back
to 1993, our success is based on safe, rapid underground development and
consistent, reliable production. We deliver a range of projects, from
large established mining operations to greenfield developments across
Australia. The services we provide range from mine development,
production, cable bolting and drilling, raise boring, shotcreting and
haulage fleet.

THE OPPORTUNITY

PYBAR is currently seeking a highly experienced and driven Development
Shift Supervisor to join our newly expanded Eloise Copper Mine Project.
Multiple points of hire will be considered across Queensland, with
flights included, and accommodation and meals provided at site.

As an Underground Development Shift Supervisor, you'll be responsible
for leading crews to deliver safe and efficient development works.
Working closely with site management and technical teams, you'll ensure
compliance with operational plans, drive performance, and maintain a
strong focus on safety, teamwork and continuous improvement underground.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

-   Provide day to day supervision, mentorship and leadership to the
    underground team

-   Monitor and supervise underground mining activities

-   Promote positive safety behaviours and help build a culture of
    accountability, ownership and safety first thinking across the site.

-   Use digital tools  to record and monitor tasks

-   Communicate effectively with PYBAR team members and relevant
    stakeholders

WHAT YOU WILL NEED TO SUCCEED

-   Minimum of 2 years\' experience in a Shift Supervisor or similar
    position 

-   Minimum of 10 years' experience in Underground Hardrock mining
    

-   Development experience .

-   Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership capabilities.

-   Current First Aid Certificate 

-   Queensland Explosives Security Clearance.

-   QMS123/G189

THE FUN STUFF

-   Career growth and development opportunities, including reimbursement
    for approved study and training

-   Exclusive access to PYBAR Perks -- employee benefits and discounts
    platform

-   Paid parental leave

-   Employee recognition programs

-   A supportive, values-led team environment where safety is truly a
    priority

-   A competitive remuneration package will be offered in line with the
    seniority of the role and the experience of the successful
    candidate.

PYBAR PERKS

-   Milestone service awards and gifts - PYBAR celebrating with you.

-   Hospital Help, have \$500 contributed towards your private health
    hospital excess.

-   Medibank corporate private health insurance discount of 9% for you
    and your family.

-   Reimbursement for prescription safety glasses up to \$350.

-   Toyota fleet discounts & novated leasing options.

-   Book your next family holiday today! Access to travel insurance &
    car hire discounts.

-   JB HiFi discounts.

-   Qantas Club and Virgin lounge membership discounts.

INCLUSION & DIVERSITY

Inclusion and diversity is important to us, which is why 'Respect' is
one of our core values. We welcome and strive for a diverse workforce
and to help us achieve this, we would love to receive applications from
females, Indigenous Australians, LGBTQI+ community members and anyone
else who would love to join our team.

HOW TO APPLY

PYBAR follows a strict pre-employment process for all successful
applicants, which will include reference checks, a national police
clearance check and Pre-employment medical. This is essential for
employment with PYBAR, and the recruitment team will guide you through
this process after a successful interview.

Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please reach out to Jess
Roach on 0427 703 019.

gaps.legislative: CMSHA s62(5)(e), MQSHA s55(5)(e) — SHMS must address psychosocial hazards. FSO developing Safe and Respectful Workplaces UoC.
gaps.note: Almost never mentioned in JDs despite increasing legislative focus. Key gap for training needs analysis.

gaps.legislative: CMSHA s30(2)(d), Schedule 3; MQSHA s27(3)(d), Schedule 2 — SSE must provide for critical controls for material unwanted events.
gaps.note: Rarely mentioned explicitly in JDs. AUSMASA currently reviewing training products for this area.

gaps.legislative: Part of SHMS requirements under both Acts. Supervisors expected to manage emergency situations on shift.
gaps.note: Supervisors are often the first responders on shift. Under-represented in JD responsibilities.

gaps.legislative: CMSHA Part 11; MQSHR s93 — Workers must be trained in relevant legislation. Supervisors must understand their statutory obligations.
terms: coal mining safety and health act
gaps.note: JDs often say 'knowledge of legislation' generically. Few specify which Acts or the depth of knowledge expected.

gaps.legislative: RIIWHS301E is a required competency for supervisors (CMSHAC/MSHAC). CMSHA, MQSHA — investigations are part of SHMS.
gaps.note: Mentioned in some JDs but often at a high level. This is a formal competency requirement.

gaps.legislative: RIIRIS301E is a required competency. Both Acts define competence as demonstrated skill and knowledge for safety.
gaps.note: JDs mention 'risk management' generally but rarely reference the formal competency unit or process.

gaps.legislative: RIICOM301E is a required competency for supervisors (CMSHAC/MSHAC).
gaps.note: Communication is listed as a skill in most JDs but rarely linked to the formal competency unit.

gaps.legislative: Brady Review Recommendations 2 and 4. Safety Reporting Survey findings on leadership skills.
gaps.note: JDs expect leadership skills but rarely require formal leadership training or qualifications.

gaps.legislative: CMSR Part 11A; MQSHR s95A, s96 — Specific regulatory duties for supervisors including checking work quality, providing time and resources.
gaps.note: Regulatory supervisor duties are rarely reflected in JD responsibilities despite being legislated.
